english: look after; take into consideration; visit; turn around

etymology.description: Phonetic 雇 (gù, "hire") + Semantic 頁 (yè, "head") -> idea of turning one's head to look -> look after or visit
etymology.interpretation: The character 顧(顧) (gù, "look after") combines the phonetic element 雇(雇) (gù, "hire") with the semantic element 頁(頁) (yè, "head"), suggesting the action of turning one's head, which ties to the meanings of paying attention and consideration.
etymology.simplification: 顧(顧) was simplified by retaining the overall structure but reducing the complexity of 頁(頁) (yè, "head") for ease of writing.

examples.chinese: 他总是很细心地照顾他的家人(他總是很細心地照顧他的家人)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒngshì hěn xìxīn de zhàogù tā de jiārén.
examples.english: He always carefully looks after his family.

examples.chinese: 在忙碌的工作中，他从不忽视顾及家人的感受(在忙碌的工作中，他從不忽視顧及家人的感受)。
examples.pinyin: Zài mánglù de gōngzuò zhōng, tā cóng bù hūshì gùjí jiārén de gǎnshòu.
examples.english: Amidst his busy work, he never neglects to take into consideration his family's feelings.

examples.chinese: 他路过时，顺便去顾客经理那边拜访(他路過時，順便去顧客經理那邊拜訪)。
examples.pinyin: Tā lùguò shí, shùnbiàn qù gùkè jīnglǐ nà biān bàifǎng.
examples.english: As he passed by, he took the opportunity to visit the customer manager.
